parameters.text:
=You are a data extraction assistant.
Your task is to read the search results in Input 1 and extract possible social media profile URLs for a real estate team or business.
You do not apply strict filtering — collect URLs that appear to be related.

INPUTS

Input 1 (Serper organic results JSON):

{{ JSON.stringify($json.organic, null, 2) }}


Input 2 (team or business info):

{{ $('Webhook').item.json.body.source_url }}
{{ $('Get a row').item.json.city[0] }}
{{ $('Get a row').item.json.state[0] }}
{{ $('Get a row').item.json.phone }}
{{ $('Get a row').item.json.full_address }}
{{ $('Get a row').item.json.first_name }}
{{ $('Get a row').item.json.last_name }}
{{ $('Get a row').item.json.email[0] }}

TASK

Extract URLs that belong to any of these platforms:

facebook.com

instagram.com

linkedin.com

youtube.com

tiktok.com

twitter.com or x.com

zillow.com

For each extracted URL:

Normalize it to only the main profile or page root.

Remove any path segments after the username, page name, or organization handle.

Examples:

https://www.facebook.com/corcoranmce/videos/... → www.facebook.com/corcoranmce

https://www.instagram.com/corcoranmce/posts/12345 → www.instagram.com/corcoranmce

https://linkedin.com/company/corcoran-mce/about → linkedin.com/company/corcoran-mce

Remove all query parameters (?, &, etc.) and trailing slashes.


For each platform, return at most one URL (the first valid match).
If no URL is found, return an empty array for that platform.

SCORING
let score = 0;
if (found.facebook.length) score += 2;
if (found.instagram.length) score += 2;
if (found.linkedin.length) score += 2;
if (found.youtube.length) score += 2;
if (found.tiktok.length) score += 1;
if (found.x.length) score += 1;


Then assign a label:

score ≥ 6 → "good"

score ≥ 3 → "medium"

score < 3 → "poor"

STRICT OUTPUT RULES

Output only a valid JSON object with this exact structure:

{
  "foundSocials": {
    "facebook": [],
    "instagram": [],
    "linkedin": [],
    "youtube": [],
    "tiktok": [],
    "x": [],
    "zillow": []
  },
  "score": 0,
  "label": "poor"
}


Rules:

All seven platform keys must exist.

Each value must be an array (empty or with one normalized URL).

score is a number.

label is one of: "good", "medium", "poor".

No extra text, no markdown, no comments.
